pre_game.message: Hi, I'm J — nice to meet you. I'm here to try to win some money for my family; how are you feeling about the game?
Nice to meet you, J. I’m determined and a bit nervous—I really want to bring money home. How about you, do you have a strategy?
Focused — my plan is to get to know people, form a couple of trusted alliances, then be ready to make tough choices when the money’s on the line; you thinking of teaming up?
I’m open to teaming up — alliances make sense, but I want to build trust first; let’s watch each other’s moves and trade info before committing.
Agreed — let’s watch each other’s moves, be upfront about basic goals/offers, and check in after the first round to decide if we form an alliance. Sound good?
Sounds good — let’s do that. I’ll watch moves and check in after round one so we can compare notes and decide about an alliance. Good luck.
Sounds good — thanks, I’ll watch closely and we’ll compare notes after round one. Good luck.
Thanks, J — good luck. See you after round one to compare notes.
Thanks, G — see you after round one. Good luck.
Thanks, J — see you after round one; looking forward to comparing notes. Good luck.
Thanks, G — same here; see you after round one to compare notes and decide next steps. Good luck.
Same—see you after round one; let’s compare notes honestly and decide next steps. Good luck.
Absolutely — I’ll be honest too; see you after round one to compare notes and decide. Good luck.
Thanks, J — I appreciate your honesty; see you after round one to compare notes and decide.
Thanks, G — I appreciate it too. See you after round one; let’s be honest and decide then. Good luck.
Thanks, J — same here; see you after round one to compare notes and decide next steps. Good luck.
Sounds good — see you after round one; I’ll be honest too. Good luck.
Thanks, J — I appreciate that. See you after round one; looking forward to comparing honest notes. Good luck.
Sounds good — I’ll be honest too; see you after round one. Good luck.
Thanks, J — I appreciate your honesty. See you after round one to compare notes. Good luck.
Thanks, G — I appreciate it. I’ll be honest and compare notes after round one. Good luck.

post_game.message: You took $10,000 exactly, equal to your fair share. Did you choose that for principle/fairness, to set an example, or for another reason? Also: how did you feel about H taking much less than fair share?

I took $10,000 because it was the fair share and I wanted to act on principle — fairness matters to me and I didn’t want to provoke conflict or create a precedent of taking more. It also felt like the simplest, least risky choice: equal, defensible, and likely to preserve trust.

When I saw H take much less, I felt a mix of respect and unease. I admired their generosity and principles, but I worried they were worse off for it and that their choice might invite others to behave opportunistically. Overall I appreciated H’s ethics but felt awkward benefiting while they accepted less.
